2604.17543.title: PoliLegalLM: A Technical Report on a Large Language Model for Political and Legal Affairs
2604.17543.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable success in general-domain tasks, yet their direct application to the legal domain remains challenging due to hallucinated legal citations, incomplete knowledge coverage, and weak structured reasoning. To address these issues, we propose PoliLegalLM, a domain-specific large language model tailored for political and legal applications. Our approach adopts a unified training framework that integrates continued pretraining, progressive supervised fine-tuning, and preference-based reinforcement learning to jointly enhance legal knowledge grounding, task alignment, and reasoning capability. We construct a large-scale, high-quality legal corpus and design a structured post-training pipeline, enabling the model to effectively learn domain-specific knowledge and adapt to diverse legal tasks. We evaluate PoliLegalLM on three representative benchmarks, including LawBench, LexEval, and a real-world dataset, PoliLegal. Experimental results demonstrate that PoliLegalLM achieves strong and consistent performance, outperforming competitive models of similar scale and remaining highly competitive with significantly larger models, while achieving the best results on real-world legal scenarios. These results highlight the effectiveness of our training paradigm and the practical value of domain-specific LLMs for real-world legal applications.
2604.17543.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在一般領域任務中取得了顯著的成功，但由於虛構的法律引用、不完整的知識覆蓋和薄弱的結構推理，其在法律領域的直接應用仍然面臨挑戰。為了解決這些問題，我們提出了 PoliLegalLM，一個專為政治和法律應用量身定制的領域特定大型語言模型。我們的方法採用了統一的訓練框架，整合了持續的預訓練、漸進的有監督微調和基於偏好的強化學習，以共同增強法律知識的基礎、任務對齊和推理能力。我們構建了一個大規模、高質量的法律語料庫，並設計了一個結構化的後訓練流程，使模型能夠有效學習領域特定知識並適應多樣的法律任務。我們在三個具有代表性的基準上評估了 PoliLegalLM，包括 LawBench、LexEval 和一個真實世界數據集 PoliLegal。實驗結果表明，PoliLegalLM 在性能上強勁且一致，超越了同類規模的競爭模型，並與顯著更大規模的模型保持高度競爭，同時在真實世界法律場景中取得了最佳結果。這些結果突顯了我們訓練範式的有效性以及領域特定 LLM 在真實世界法律應用中的實際價值。
2604.17543.author: Yuting Huang et.al.
2604.17543.authors: Yuting Huang, Yinghao Hu, Qian Xiao, Wenlin Zhong, Yiquan Wu, Taishi Zhou, Moke Chen, Changlong Sun, Kun Kuang, Fei Wu
